text: What actually happens the exact millisec
ond you click a URL short ener ?
Let 's trace the path of a single click .
Step one , the request .
Your browser makes a request , but it doesn
't go straight to a server .
It hits a load bal ancer .
Why a load bal ancer ?
Because if a link goes viral , millions of
clicks happen at once and a single server
would melt .
The load bal ancer acts as a traffic cop
, rooting your click to the least busy server
Step two , the cache .
The server receives the request and needs
to find the original long URL .
But it does not go to the database first
It checks an in - memory cache like Red is
If the link is viral , it is already in the
cache making the redirect lightning fast
Step three , the database .
If the link is not in the cache , the server
queries a no SQL database .
A short ener is just a massive key value
dictionary .
The server finds the long link , saves it
back into Red is for the next person , and
moves to the final step .
Step four , the redirect .
The server sends back an HTTP status code
You must use a 302 temporary redirect , not
A 301 saves the link on the user 's computer
, so you lose tracking .
A 302 forces them to hit your server every
time , which is exactly how you track analytics
and make money .
The browser reads the 302 and instantly redirect
s the user .
